opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I keep seeing bright red blood in the toilet bowl, and I'm terrified I've got bowel cancer.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The bleeding has been coming and going for about three weeks now and it's really frightening me — I want to know what's going on and whether I need to be seen.

symptom_narrative.duration: on and off for three weeks
symptom_narrative.character: bright red blood coating the poo and dripping into the toilet bowl afterwards
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: it's only ever a small amount — a few drops or a smear, never a big gush, but it keeps happening and it scares me
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: from my back passage when I open my bowels
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: comes and goes, a few times a week, always when I go to the toilet
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing I've tried really changes it
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: I've noticed my bowels have been a bit more constipated than usual
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: I've been feeling a bit more tired than normal, but no bad tummy pain, no vomiting, and I haven't lost any weight that I know of
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm still able to do everything as usual, but I'm anxious and worrying about it constantly

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: generally healthy, no ongoing bowel problems that I know of

family_history_as_reported.narrative: I'm not really sure about bowel problems in the family — nothing I've been told about.

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: very worried about cancer; the fear is the main reason I'm calling tonight

objective_data_offered.value: photo the patient offers of the toilet bowl showing bright red blood coating the stool with a few drops in the water
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: Patient-offered image of colour/appearance only; cannot localise the bleeding source or quantify blood loss, and cannot substitute for endoscopic visualisation.

objective_data_offered.value: patient on video looks well and is talking in full sentences, appears anxious but not pale, sweaty or breathless
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: Video impression only; pallor and haemodynamic status cannot be reliably assessed without a device or examination.
